plain_english: A positive SHAP value pushes the risk score up. A negative SHAP value pushes the risk score down.

global_summary.plain_english: Past late payments are the strongest average driver of risk.
Using most of the available credit usually pushes risk up.
Stable income often pushes risk down.
Longer employment history often pushes risk down.
Suspicious feature. It is only known after the loan decision.

local_cases.plain_english: The model was wrong here. It overreacted to two late payments and high credit utilization, even though stable income and employment history lowered the risk.
The model and reality agree. The explanation is still useful because it tells a human which facts mattered.

known_trap.why_bad: This count only exists after a loan decision and follow-up process. If the model uses it during approval, it is peeking into the future.
known_trap.safe_fix: Remove the feature, retrain, and audit all features for decision-time availability.
